Monday, May 25, 2026 Daily Digest

Google AI Mode Hits 1B Users, Publicis Acquires LiveRamp for $2.5B, TikTok Shop Eyes $23B

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Google AI Mode Hits 1B Users, Redesigns Search After 25 Years

Google announced at I/O 2026 that AI Mode has crossed one billion monthly users, with queries doubling every quarter. The search box now accepts text, images, files, videos, and browser tabs as inputs. Instead of blue links, Google generates custom summary pages and opens conversations with AI Mode. The kicker: brands cited in AI Overviews earn 35% more organic clicks than competitors. This is the biggest shift in search visibility since PageRank — and marketers need to optimize for answers, not just rankings.

Read on B2the7 →
2

Publicis Acquires LiveRamp for $2.5B to Power Agentic Marketing

Publicis Groupe is acquiring LiveRamp, the identity resolution platform, in an all-cash deal worth $2.546B (30% premium). CEO Arthur Sadoun described it as a move to "become a leader in data co-creation" and position for the agentic era. The signal is clear: first-party data infrastructure is the most strategic marketing investment right now. AI agents need quality data to perform — poor data infrastructure limits what AI can do for you.

Read on B2the7 →
3

Google Marketing Live: Gemini Becomes the Operating Layer

At Marketing Live 2026, Google positioned Gemini not as a feature but as the operational intelligence layer coordinating campaigns, measurement, commerce, and customer engagement across its entire ecosystem. New capabilities include "journey-aware bidding" that understands complex lead gen journeys, Conversational Discovery ads, and "Ask Advisor" — a cross-product AI collaborator. The message: AI is evolving from a tool into an operational layer that coordinates everything.

Read on CMSWire →
4

TikTok Shop Projected to Hit $23B in US Sales

TikTok Shop is projected to hit $23.41B in US e-commerce sales in 2026 — a 48% increase YoY. That would put it ahead of Target, Costco, Best Buy, and Kroger in US e-commerce volume. More than half (51%) of US social buyers are expected to make at least one purchase on TikTok this year. Live-commerce GMV alone is growing 42% YoY. The platform has crossed a threshold where it's no longer experimental — brands not building a TikTok commerce presence are ceding ground that's becoming hard to recover.

Read on B2the7 →
5

Pacvue Connects Retail Media Data to AI Assistants via MCP

Pacvue introduced Report MCP, connecting retail media data directly with AI assistants like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Copilot using Anthropic's Model Context Protocol. Instead of exporting reports, cleaning spreadsheets, and uploading to chatbots, marketers can ask plain-language questions and get live data. This signals the industry moving beyond isolated AI tools toward connected, agentic systems that orchestrate workflows across platforms.

Read on MarTech →

💡 My Take

The headline of the week: Google AI Mode hitting 1B users with 35% more clicks for brands cited in AI Overviews. This changes the game. It's no longer about ranking — it's about being named in the AI-generated answer. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) isn't optional anymore. Meanwhile, Publicis paying $2.5B for LiveRamp confirms what we've been seeing: first-party data infrastructure is the foundation AI-driven marketing runs on.
